action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 10 completed training a 512h/4l model for 35 epochs (batch_size=64) in ~30 min. Best validation loss was 0.3600 at epoch 25, which is better than the previous best model's 0.3665 (iteration 6, which scored mae_60min=3.9361). The architecture is identical to the proven iteration-6 model (adaptive graph + GLU + skip connections, no attention). With only ~22 minutes remaining, training a new model from scratch is too risky and time-consuming (~30 min). Analysis: The current checkpoint is very likely to improve upon the 3.9361 baseline because it achieves lower validation loss with the same architecture. Decision: Immediately submit the current best checkpoint for evaluation. Run a quick load sanity-check first to ensure the checkpoint isn't corrupted, then submit eval.
action.command: python -c "import torch; ckpt=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', weights_only=False); print('Checkpoint OK. Keys:', list(ckpt.keys()))"
